Project Name: Lanjigarh Alumina Refinery Expansion (2 → 5 MTPA)
Scope: Multi-phase brownfield expansion: 3.5 MTPA commissioned Apr 2024; full 5 MTPA reached Dec 2025 — now India's largest alumina refinery and one of world's largest outside China

Project Name: BALCO Korba Smelter Expansion — 525 kA Potline (0.435 MTPA)
Scope: New 525 kA high-amperage potline at Korba; takes BALCO to 1 MTPA (India's largest 525 kA smelter, >20% of India's aluminium output); first metal Oct 2025

Project Name: Jharsuguda Cast House-4 & Value-Added Products Expansion
Scope: New 180 KTPA Primary Foundry Alloy line + 2×125 KTPA billet lines at Jharsuguda; PFA line commissioned Feb 2025; billet lines achieved Jun 2025; lifts VAP share

Project Name: Jharsuguda Aluminium Smelter Expansion (1.75 → 1.8 MTPA)
Scope: Debottlenecking: adding 66 pots in Smelter-2; Odisha SPCB consent issued Apr 3, 2026; 5-year window to commence construction; commissioning expected FY2028-30

Project Name: HZL Rajasthan Underground Mines Expansion (10 → 15 MTPA ore)
Scope: Multi-mine underground expansion and mill upgrades at Rampura Agucha, Sindesar Khurd, Rajpura Dariba, Zawar, Kayad and Bamania Kalan; investment plan announced Apr 2026 over 3 years

Project Name: Rampura Agucha 10 MTPA Zinc Tailings Reprocessing Plant
Scope: First-of-its-kind 10 MTPA zinc tailings reprocessing plant to recover zinc and silver from historical tailings at Rampura Agucha; HZL board approved Aug 2025; 28-month implementation

Project Name: HZL 250 KTPA Integrated Zinc Metal Complex — Debari
Scope: New 250 KTPA integrated zinc metal complex at Debari; part of HZL 2X growth plan to nearly double zinc-lead-silver capacity; 36-month completion from board approval

Project Name: Bamnia Kalan & Sindesar Kalan Underground Lead-Zinc Mine Expansion
Scope: Underground lead-zinc ore expansion from 0.12 MTPA to 1.5 MTPA ROM; portal excavation started; infrastructure orders placed; ore production expected post-FY27

Project Name: Bicholim Mineral Block-1 Iron Ore Mine — 3 MTPA (Goa)
Scope: Restart of iron ore mining at Bicholim Block-1 after 6-year halt; first post-2018 auction mine to become operational in Goa; EC granted Jan 2024; mining operations formally commenced Apr 4, 2024

Project Name: Janthakal Iron Ore Mine — Preferred Bidder (G3 block, Karnataka)
Scope: 71.16 ha G3-stage iron ore block in Chitradurga; ~12.51 Mt geological resources; Vedanta declared preferred bidder Jul 2025; mining lease grant pending statutory approvals

Project Name: Mangala Field ASP Enhanced Oil Recovery — India's Largest Commercial ASP
Scope: Full-field Alkaline-Surfactant-Polymer (ASP) flooding at Mangala (MWP-1 & 19); boosts recovery from ~40% to up to 60%; ~20% additional oil from treated area; India's largest commercial ASP injection

Project Name: RJ-ON-90/1 ASP & Advanced Recovery Programme — MBA Fields
Scope: Multi-field EOR across Mangala, Bhagyam and Aishwariya; ~$1 billion ASP investment 2025-28; part of $3-4 billion upstream capex; ONGC 30% partner; PSC extended to May 2030

Project Name: Cairn OALP Round-IX — 7 New Exploration Blocks (West Coast)
Scope: 4 onshore + 3 shallow-water blocks in Cambay, Saurashtra and Mumbai basins; takes Cairn portfolio to 69 blocks (~73,000 sq km); accelerated exploration 2025-27 to contribute 50% of India's domestic crude

Project Name: KG Basin Deepwater Exploration — 5-6 Wells by 2026
Scope: Deepwater exploration in KG-DWHP-2017/1 block; 3D CSEM surveys completed; 5-6 exploration wells planned by 2026; part of $3-4 billion upstream capex plan

Project Name: Meenakshi Energy 1,000 MW Coal Power Plant — Full Revival
Scope: IBC acquisition Jan 2023 (₹1,440 Cr); Phase I 300 MW already operational; Phase II 700 MW (2×350 MW) commissioned Aug 2025; plant now fully operational at 1,000 MW as IPP

Project Name: Vedanta Chhattisgarh Thermal Power Plant (VLCTPP) — 1,200 MW
Scope: Half-built 2×600 MW coal thermal plant acquired from Athena Chhattisgarh via insolvency; completed under Vedanta; Unit-1 (600 MW) commissioned Aug 2025; Unit-2 to follow

Project Name: HZL — Serentica 450→530 MW Round-the-Clock Renewable Power (Captive)
City: Bikaner (generation) → HZL units in Udaipur, Bhilwara, Rajsamand
Scope: Captive solar-wind-storage RTC renewable power supply to HZL operations; 180 MW Bikaner solar park commissioned Jul 2024; PDA enhanced to 530 MW; full hybrid system by 2027

Project Name: HZL 0.5 MTPA DAP Fertiliser Plant — Udaipur
Scope: Greenfield 0.5 MTPA Di-Ammonium Phosphate (DAP) plant at Udaipur using rock phosphate from HZL's Maton mine; part of ₹8,000 Cr 3-year Rajasthan investment plan announced Apr 2026

Project Name: Vedanta Semiconductors & Displays — Dholera Fab (₹1.54 lakh crore MoU)
Scope: Proposed integrated semiconductor fab (28nm, 300mm wafer) + Gen-8 display fab + OSAT at Dholera SIR; MoU Sep 2022 with Gujarat Govt; Foxconn JV dissolved; Vedanta seeking new tech and equity partners

Project Name: Sterlite Copper 'Green Copper' Smelter Revival — Thoothukudi
Scope: Proposal to revive closed Sterlite Copper smelter as a new 'green copper' facility; Vedanta filed petition Dec 2025 in Madras High Court for expert committee evaluation; judicial and regulatory approval pending

Project Name: Vedanta Aluminium Park Jharsuguda + 3 MTPA Aluminium Plant — Odisha
Scope: 253-acre aluminium industrial park near Jharsuguda for 100+ MSMEs + proposed 3 MTPA greenfield aluminium smelter in Rayagada/Dhenkanal; MoU with Odisha Govt Jan 2025; 3-4 year build horizon

Project Name: Greenfield Integrated Steel & Manufacturing Complex — Paradip
Project Owner (Subsidiary): JSW Steel / JSW Utkal Steel Ltd (Developer) — EPC not yet disclosed
Scope: 13.2 MTPA integrated green steel plant with captive power plant, captive jetties, cement unit, township and associated infrastructure; land ~2,958 acres; multi-phase development over several years

Project Name: JSW Vijayanagar Metallics — 5 MTPA Brownfield Steel Expansion
Project Owner (Subsidiary): JSW Vijayanagar Metallics Ltd (JVML) — Multiple EPC packages; not fully disclosed
Scope: Brownfield addition of 4.5 MTPA blast furnace, 2×350-ton steel melt shops and 5 MTPA hot strip mill at Vijayanagar; HSM, BF, sinter & RMHS commissioned Mar 2024; SMS ramp-up through FY25; raises Vijayanagar to ~18 MTPA

Project Name: Dolvi Works Phase-III Brownfield Expansion (10 → 15 MTPA)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): JSW Steel Ltd — In-house engineering + package contractors; not centrally disclosed
Scope: Brownfield expansion adding 5 MTPA at Dolvi with associated coke ovens, pellet plant and logistics upgrades; part of JSW Steel's ₹61,863 Cr India capex FY26-28; significant share earmarked for Dolvi

Project Name: Iron Ore Mining & Pellet / Slurry Logistics — Odisha (Multiple Mines)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): JSW Steel & Mining subsidiaries — EPC for individual mines/pipeline not fully disclosed
Scope: Multiple iron ore mines in Keonjhar & Sundargarh districts; 2 × 8 MTPA pellet plants and 30 MTPA slurry pipeline to supply Vijayanagar; ~1.1 billion tonne aggregate ore reserves; FY27-28 target

Project Name: Kutehr Hydroelectric Power Plant — 240 MW (3×80 MW)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): JSW Energy Kutehr Ltd (wholly-owned step-down) — Civil & electro-mechanical EPC packages; not all named publicly
Scope: Run-of-river hydro plant on Ravi River; 35-year PPA with Haryana Power Purchase Centre at ₹4.50/kWh levelised ceiling tariff; completed in under 6 years — one of India's fastest greenfield hydro builds

Project Name: SECI Tranche X Wind Power Project — 450 MW
Project Owner (Subsidiary): JSW Renew Energy Two Ltd (JSW Energy step-down) — Turbine OEM/EPC not named
Scope: 450 MW ISTS-connected wind project under SECI Tranche X with 25-year PPA; 300 MW Tuticorin fully commissioned Sep 2024; 138 MW of 150 MW Dharapuram commissioned by late 2025; remaining 12 MW expected by end-FY25

Project Name: 1.0 GWh Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) — SECI Awarded
Project Owner (Subsidiary): JSW Energy / JSW Neo Energy — EPC not publicly disclosed
State: Multiple (ISTS locations — exact sites not disclosed)
Scope: Two 500 MWh grid-scale BESS installations developed under Build-Own-Operate model against SECI tender; provides 2-hour discharge for grid balancing and RE integration; construction commenced Jun 2024

Project Name: Multi-Site 3.0 GWh BESS + 26.4 GWh Pumped Storage Portfolio
Project Owner (Subsidiary): JSW Energy / JSW Neo Energy — Individual EPC partners to be appointed per project
Scope: JSW Energy's locked-in storage portfolio of 29.4 GWh: 26.4 GWh in pumped storage and 3.0 GWh in BESS across multiple states; target 40 GWh by FY30; various projects at pre-construction or early construction stage

Project Name: 25 MW Green Hydrogen Plant — Vijayanagar
Project Owner (Subsidiary): JSW Energy / JSW Neo Energy (Developer) — Technology providers not fully disclosed
Scope: 25 MW electrolysis-based green hydrogen facility (~3,800 TPA H₂) for JSW Steel Vijayanagar's green steel and decarbonisation programme; trial runs near complete as of Oct 2025; key part of JSW's net-zero roadmap

Project Name: KG-DWN-98/2 Cluster-II Deepwater Development
Scope: Deepwater gas/condensate development; FPSO Armada Sterling V; Subsea SPS+SURF EPC; First oil Jan 2024; production ramp-up ongoing

Project Name: Mumbai High North Redevelopment Phase-IV (MHN RD-IV)
City: Arabian Sea, ~160 km west of Mumbai
Scope: Offshore redevelopment of Mumbai High North field; platform upgrade and subsea works; dedicated by PM Mar 2024; cost overrun ~23%

Project Name: Daman Upside Development Project (DUDP)
City: ~180 km NW of Mumbai; ~80 km south of Pipavav
Scope: Western offshore gas development; platform B-12-24P; gas flowing to Hazira plant from Mar 29, 2026; completed in <2 years from award

Project Name: Subsea Pipeline Replacements (PRP-VIII & PRP-9)
Scope: Replacement of aging subsea pipelines across Mumbai High, Neelam and Heera fields; PRP-9 EPC to Lamprell

Project Name: Jharia Coal Bed Methane (CBM) Project
Scope: CBM gas extraction from Jharia coalfields; JV with Coal India; gas to be monetised from coal seams

Project Name: DSF-II Integrated Development (4 Areas)
Scope: Discovered Small Field-II integrated development across 4 offshore continental shelf areas; Board approved Aug 2024

Project Name: Andaman Basin Stratigraphic Well (AND-P-1)
Scope: Ultra-deepwater exploratory well 267 nautical miles from Andaman Islands; technical SME support from BP; spudded Jan 27, 2026

Project Name: HPCL Visakh Refinery Modernisation Project (VRMP) + RUF
Scope: Expanded refinery from 8.33 to 15 MMTPA; added BS-VI capability; LC-Max residue hydrocracking unit (3.55 MMTPA); RUF commissioned early 2026

Project Name: MRPL Iso-Butyl Benzene (IBB) Demo Plant
Scope: Demonstration-scale IBB production plant at MRPL Mangalore refinery; in-house development

Project Name: MRPL Power System Upgradation
Scope: Grid power system upgrade at MRPL refinery; KPTCL grid linkage for improved power reliability

Project Name: MRPL Bio-ATF (Sustainable Aviation Fuel) Demo
Scope: Demonstration plant for Bio-ATF / Sustainable Aviation Fuel production at MRPL; green aviation fuel initiative

Project Name: MRPL New Petrochemical Complex (West Coast)
Scope: Large petrochemical complex integrated with MRPL refinery; configuration under study; shifted focus from refinery expansion; EPC not yet appointed

Project Name: Prayagraj Refinery-cum-Petrochem Complex
Scope: Greenfield refinery and petrochemical complex at Naini/Bara; ONGC + BPCL JV; part of O2C expansion plan

Project Name: Jamnagar Refinery & Petrochemical Project
Scope: Greenfield refinery and petchem complex at Jamnagar; site selection stage; part of ONGC group's 8 MMTPA petchem target by 2030

Project Name: TP Solar 4.3 GW Cell & Module Manufacturing Plant
Project Owner (Subsidiary): Tata Power Solar Systems Ltd (TP Solar)
Scope: 4.3 GW solar cell & module manufacturing plant. Module line: Oct 2023. Cell line: 09-09-2024 (2 GW). Full 4.3 GW capacity target FY26. India's largest integrated solar manufacturing facility.

Project Name: Bhivpuri Pumped Hydro Storage Project (1,000 MW)
Scope: 1,000 MW pumped hydro storage project. Board approval Oct 2024. ₹2,470 Cr civil & hydro-mechanical EPC contract to HCC–Tata Projects JV (Mar 2025). Part of 2,800 MW PSP portfolio with Shirawta.

Project Name: Shirawta Pumped Hydro Storage Project (1,800 MW)
Scope: 1,800 MW pumped hydro storage project. Investment: ₹11,000 Cr. CEO confirmed Jul 2026 start and 5-year completion timeline at Nov 2025 briefing. Part of Tata Power's 2,800 MW PSP portfolio.

Project Name: West Bokaro Coal Mine Expansion – 10 MTPA Coking Coal Washery
Scope: New 10 MTPA coking coal washery at West Bokaro. Adds to existing 6.5 MTPA capacity. 20-year washery life from commissioning. Pre-feasibility filed with MoEF&CC Feb 2022.

Project Name: Gandhalpada Iron Ore Mine, Odisha (10 MTPA)
Construction Start Date: Not Disclosed (Mining lease not yet executed; forest diversion approval pending)
Scope: 10 MTPA iron ore mine (314.37 Mt resources; 33-year mine life). LoI for 50-year lease over 241.1 ha issued 28-10-2021. Forest clearance (216.875 ha) applied. Production target FY29 to replace expiring leases.

Project Name: Noamundi / Joda / Katamati Iron Ore Mine Expansion (40 → 55 MTPA)
Scope: Multi-site iron ore capacity expansion from 40 MTPA to 55 MTPA across OMQ division. Investment: ~₹10,000 Cr (~US$1.18 Bn). Technology, logistics and debottlenecking upgrades. No single commissioning date – phased ramp-up.

Project Name: Tata Group 1 GW AI Data Centre Programme (TCS HyperVault + OpenAI + AMD)
Expected Commissioning Date: Not Disclosed (Phased: 100 MW initial phase, scaling to 1 GW; timeline not published)
Scope: 1 GW AI-optimised data centre programme. Phase 1: 100 MW via TCS HyperVault. Partnerships: OpenAI, AMD, TPG. Total capex: ~US$6.5-7 Bn. Announced Feb 2026. Construction timelines and site locations not yet disclosed.

Project Name: Dahej Petrochemical & Polyester Value Chain Expansion (3 MMTPA PTA + 1 MMTPA PET)
Scope: 3 MMTPA PTA + 1 MMTPA PET integrated polyester value chain. Part of ₹75,000 Cr O2C capex. Dahej prefeasibility: ~₹21,200 Cr for PTA/PET/Carbon Fibre/CPVC/utilities at site.

Project Name: Vinyl Chain (PVC/CPVC) Capacity Expansion – 1.5 MMTPA
Scope: 1.5 MMTPA PVC/CPVC across 3 sites. Vinyl-chain capacity more than tripling. Part of ₹75,000 Cr O2C capex. Nagothane projected full commissioning 31-12-2028.

Project Name: Carbon Fibre & Advanced Materials Complex – Hazira
Scope: India's first large-scale carbon fibre plant (20,000 MTPA Phase 1). Part of ₹75,000 Cr O2C/new materials capex. Further ramp through 2026-27.

Project Name: KG-D6 Incremental Gas Development – R-Cluster & Satellite Cluster Additional Wells
Scope: 3 additional wells in R-Cluster + 1 in Satellite Cluster. KG-D6 already producing. Incremental 4-5 mmscmd output targeted. Capex not publicly disclosed. MJ Field first gas: 30-06-2023 (achieved).

Project Name: Dhirubhai Ambani Green Energy Giga Complex – Solar Cell & Module Gigafactory (10-20 GW)
Scope: 10-20 GW annual solar cell & module manufacturing (quartz-to-module). HJT technology (REC-based), 720 Wp modules. Part of ₹75,000 Cr (~US$10 Bn) new energy capex. BIS-certified production underway.

Project Name: Jamnagar Battery Gigafactory – 40 GWh (Scalable to 100 GWh)
Scope: 40 GWh/year integrated battery manufacturing (scalable to 100 GWh). Part of Jamnagar Giga Complex. US$10 Bn new energy investment envelope.

Project Name: Electrolyser Gigafactory – Jamnagar (3 GW/year capacity)
Scope: 3 GW/year electrolyser manufacturing plant within Jamnagar Giga Complex to support green hydrogen production. Part of US$10 Bn new energy capex.

Project Name: Kutch Ultra-Large Solar & Green Hydrogen Project (~550,000 acres)
Scope: One of world's largest single-site solar projects (~550,000 acres). Feeds green hydrogen chain targeting 3 MMTPA by 2032. Linked to Jamnagar Giga Complex and Kandla export corridor. Capex not separately disclosed.

Project Name: Green Hydrogen & Green Chemicals Complex – Kandla
Scope: 2,000-acre integrated green hydrogen and green chemicals complex (green ammonia, methanol, SAF). Marine/land infrastructure linked to Kutch solar and Jamnagar Giga Complex. Capex not publicly disclosed.

Project Name: Chennai MAA10 Data Centre – 20 MW (on 100 MW campus)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): Digital Connexion JV (RIL + Brookfield + Digital Realty)
Scope: 20 MW Phase 1 on 100 MW campus. Mukesh Ambani confirmed operations at TN Global Investors Meet Jan 2024. Further expansion to 100 MW in subsequent phases. Capex not separately disclosed.

Project Name: Mumbai Chandivali Data Centre Campus – ~40 MW
Project Owner (Subsidiary): Digital Connexion JV (RIL + Brookfield + Digital Realty)
Scope: 40 MW data centre on 2.15-acre land parcel in Chandivali, Mumbai. Part of Digital Connexion JV's India-wide DC investment plan. Capex not disclosed.

Project Name: Jamnagar AI Data Centre – Phase 1 (120 MW)
Scope: AI-native data centre campus. Construction confirmed at India AI Impact Summit 2026. Phase 1: 120 MW. Integrated with Jamnagar Giga Complex green energy infrastructure.

Project Name: Visakhapatnam AI-Native Data Centre Campus – 1 GW
Project Owner (Subsidiary): Digital Connexion JV (RIL + Brookfield + Digital Realty)
Scope: 1 GW AI-native DC campus on ~400 acres. MoU with AP Economic Development Board (2025). Investment ~US$11 Bn (~₹98,000 Cr) by 2030. Pre-construction / planning phase.

Project Name: Telangana Super Thermal Power Project Stage-I (2×800 MW = 1,600 MW)
Scope: 1,600 MW ultra-supercritical (USC) coal plant. Efficiency ~42%, reduced CO₂/kWh. 85% power allocated to Telangana state. Technology: USC boiler-turbine-generator.

Project Name: Lara Super Thermal Power Project Stage-II (2×800 MW = 1,600 MW)
Scope: 1,600 MW USC/USC+ expansion on Stage-I land. Full-scope EPC by BHEL (BTG + BoP). Designed for biomass co-firing and advanced emission controls. Investment: ₹15,529.99 Cr. Coal: Talaipalli block.

Project Name: Talcher Thermal Power Project Stage-III (2×660 MW = 1,320 MW)
Scope: 1,320 MW supercritical coal expansion replacing old units. Full-scope EPC by BHEL (BTG + BoP). Board-approved capex: ~₹9,785 Cr.

Project Name: North Karanpura Super Thermal Power Project (3×660 MW = 1,980 MW)
Scope: 1,980 MW supercritical coal plant. India's first large air-cooled condenser (ACC) — reduces water use to 1/3 of conventional plants. Pithead coal supply. Supports eastern regional grid.

Project Name: Khavda-I Solar PV Project (1,255 MW)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): NTPC Renewable Energy Ltd (subsidiary of NTPC Green Energy Ltd)
Scope: 1,255 MW ground-mounted solar PV under CPSU Phase-II Tranche-III (ISTS-connected). Developed by NTPC REL for self-use. Part of NGEL's 60 GW by 2032 target.

Project Name: Nokh Solar PV Project (245 MW)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): NTPC Renewable Energy Ltd / NTPC Green Energy Ltd
Scope: 245 MW solar PV under CPSU scheme. Feeds NTPC internal and ISTS demand. Contributes to NGEL's 32+ GW RE portfolio.

Project Name: Chhattargarh Solar PV Project (150 MW)
Scope: 150 MW grid-connected solar PV. First operational project of NTPC REL. Part of NTPC REL's ~3.5 GW RE under execution as of 31-03-2024.

Project Name: Kadapa Solar PV Project (250 MW)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): ONGC NTPC Green Pvt Ltd (JV of NTPC Green Energy Ltd & ONGC)
Construction Start Date: Not Disclosed (developed by Ayana Kadapa Renewable Power Pvt Ltd)
Scope: 250 MW solar PV. Developed by Ayana Kadapa Renewable Power (ONGC-NTPC Green JV subsidiary). 25-year PPA with SECI at ₹2.71/kWh. Contributes ~9.3 GW NGEL installed RE capacity.

Project Name: Tehri Pumped Storage Plant (4×250 MW = 1,000 MW Variable-Speed PSP)
City: Tehri Garhwal (Tehri & Koteshwar reservoirs, Bhagirathi River)
Scope: India's first variable-speed PSP (4×250 MW). Uses Tehri HPP as upper and Koteshwar HEP as lower reservoir. Provides flexible peaking power and grid balancing. Project cost: ~₹8,339 Cr.

Project Name: Vishnugad Pipalkoti Hydro Electric Project – VPHEP (4×111 MW = 444 MW)
Scope: 444 MW run-of-river HEP. 65 m diversion dam, 13.4 km headrace tunnel, underground powerhouse. Generates ~1,665 GWh/year for northern grid. ~40-50% physical progress. Partly financed by World Bank.

Project Name: Mahi Banswara Rajasthan Atomic Power Project – MBRAPP (4×700 MW = 2,800 MW PHWR)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): ASHVINI – Anushakti Vidhyut Nigam Ltd (JV: NPCIL 51% + NTPC 49%)
Expected Commissioning Date: Not Disclosed (typical fleet-mode PHWR: ~6-7 years per twin-unit block; no binding COD published)
Scope: 4×700 MW indigenous IPHWR-700 PHWR. Two phases: Units 1&2 and Units 3&4. Estimated cost: ~₹42,000 Cr. First nuclear project by two CPSEs via JV. India's fleet-mode nuclear expansion.

Project Name: Chutka Madhya Pradesh Atomic Power Project (2×700 MW PHWR)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): ASHVINI – Anushakti Vidhyut Nigam Ltd (JV: NPCIL 51% + NTPC 49%)
Scope: 2×700 MW IPHWR-700 indigenous PHWR. Estimated cost: ~₹25,000 Cr. Part of fleet-mode nuclear expansion under ASHVINI JV alongside Mahi Banswara.

Project Name: Standalone BESS at Gadarwara & Solapur (250 MW / 500 MWh)
Expected Commissioning Date: Not Disclosed (commissioning post-tender award; ~12-year EPC + O&M term)
Scope: 250 MW / 500 MWh grid-connected standalone BESS at two NTPC sites. Two 2-hour daily cycles, ≥80% round-trip efficiency. Supports Gadarwara solar plant (MP) and Solapur coal station (MH). Includes design, supply, grid integration, and long-term O&M.

Project Name: NTPC 4.7 GWh Battery Energy Storage System Program (Multi-Site)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): NTPC Ltd / NTPC Renewable Energy Ltd
City: Mouda, Kudgi, Ramagundam, Solapur, Barh, Nabinagar, Simhadri, Bikaner (Multiple Sites)
Expected Commissioning Date: Not Disclosed (staggered commissioning post-award at each site; 11-year O&M term)
Scope: Portfolio of utility-scale BESS totalling 4.7 GWh (~₹5,822 Cr capex). Winners include GR Infraprojects. Sites: Mouda, Kudgi, Ramagundam, Solapur, Barh, Nabinagar, Simhadri + 250 MW/1,000 MWh at Bikaner RE. Firms RE output and provides peak/ancillary grid services.

Project Name: Khavda Renewable Energy Park (30 GW)
Scope: 30 GW solar + wind RE park. World's largest single-site RE park. EPC: SWREL, Bondada Engineering, KPI Green. ₹1,50,000 Cr committed.

Project Name: Khavda BESS Deployment (1,376 MWh)
Scope: 1,376 MWh BESS deployed at Khavda RE Park to support grid stability. EPC: Adani Green Energy.

Project Name: Panaura Pumped Hydro Storage (1,250 MW)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): Adani Green Energy Ltd (AGEL) / Adani Saur Urja
Scope: 1,250 MW pumped storage hydro awarded by UPPCL. 40-year commitment, 6-year execution timeline. Pre-construction/planning stage.

Project Name: Gandikota Pumped Hydro (1,800 MW)
Scope: 1,800 MW pumped hydro. ANDRITZ awarded electromechanical package Q2 2025. Civil works not yet started.

Project Name: Mahan Super Thermal Power Expansion (2×800 MW)
Project Owner (Subsidiary): Adani Power Ltd / Mahan Energen Ltd
Scope: 1,600 MW ultra-supercritical brownfield expansion at existing 1,200 MW Mahan plant. Part of ₹1,20,000 Cr capex plan.

Project Name: Pirpainti Thermal Power Plant (3×800 MW)
Expected Commissioning Date: First unit ~48 months; full plant ~60 months from appointed date
Scope: 2,400 MW greenfield USCTPP for Bihar DISCOMs. EPC: BTL EPC Ltd. PSA signed 13-Sep-2025. Investment ~₹590 Cr (BTL package). Total ~₹26,482 Cr project.

Project Name: Pan-India CNG & PNG Infrastructure Expansion
Scope: Expansion of CNG stations and PNG pipeline networks across 50+ GAs in 13 states. Investment ₹16,000 Cr over 7 years. 600+ CNG stations already operational.

Project Name: Suliyari Coal Mine Expansion (7 MTPA)
Scope: 7 MTPA captive coal mine under Adani as MDO. Project value ₹386.20 Cr. Operational and in extraction phase.

Project Name: Badam Coal Mine (MDO)
Scope: Coal mine developed by Adani Enterprises as MDO. Commissioned Feb 2025.

Project Name: Navi Mumbai Hyperscale Data Centre (1 GW)
Scope: 1 GW hyperscale DC infrastructure. MoU signed Jan 2024 (WEF Davos) with Maharashtra Govt. Investment ₹50,000 Cr over 10 years.

Project Name: Pune Data Centre (250 MW)
Scope: 250 MW data centre campus in Pune. Part of Maharashtra 1 GW DC program under MoU Jan 2024.

Project Name: Hyderabad Data Centre (600 MW)
Scope: 600 MW hyperscale data centre. Phase 1 commissioned Oct 2024. AdaniConneX JV development.

Project Name: Visakhapatnam AI Data Centre Campus
Scope: India's largest AI-oriented DC campus. Adani-Google partnership announced Oct 2025. Part of ~US$15 Bn (~₹1,25,000 Cr) multi-year India investment.
